text: Bustling

A landmark

To immerse yourself
To be worth the trip
A getaway
To stand out
It must be awe-inspiring in person.
With those crowds, it must be a famous landmark.
It is so empty, it might be off the beaten track.
There is no one here, it can't be a tourist hotspot.
Looking at this old photo, the place must have been awe-inspiring.
You have travelled so much, Pricila. Of all the places you have seen, which one really stands out?
A tiny village in the mountains, completely off the beaten track. It was unspoiled and picturesque, a real hidden gem.
That sounds peaceful. It must have been hard to reach if it is so far from everything.
It was, but it was worth the trip. I got to immerse myself in the local life. What about you, any big landmark you loved?
Honestly, the most famous one I saw was a bit overrated. The bustling, sprawling city around it was far more interesting.
I know exactly what you mean. The crowds can't be the real reason to go. For me, the quiet getaways are always the most awe-inspiring.

So, here is my honest review of last month's trip. The famous coastal landmark everyone talks about? Frankly, it was a little overrated, and the crowds were exhausting. But then we drove two hours inland and found the real story. The old town there is a hidden gem, completely off the beaten track, so most tourists never see it. The streets are calm and unspoiled, the stone houses are picturesque, and the valley behind them is genuinely awe-inspiring. If you only have time for one thing, skip the famous spot. The quiet countryside was the most memorable part of the whole journey, and it was absolutely worth the trip.

When I was younger, I used to rush. I would arrive in a new city and try to tick every famous landmark off a list, photo after photo, never really stopping. These days, I travel differently. I no longer chase the crowds. Instead, I like to immerse myself slowly in the local life. I sit in one quiet square, I talk to the people, I let a place reveal itself. A destination does not have to be famous to be worth the trip. For me, a place is special when it stands out and stays with me, long after I come home. That is what a life of travel really means.
